methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ABL Bio is a public-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, founder Sang Hoon Lee, having raised over $5B through IPO and licensing agreements. The comparable universe consists of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Two standard valuation methods cannot be applied here. Peer-median analysis fails due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Biotech & Health peer set; while 16 same-sector peers exist broadly, none of the tighter filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier) yielded the minimum three datapoints required for statistical validity. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable because target revenue is not on file—a standard constraint for private companies and one that prevents application of the sector P/S median of 6.6x observed across the three public tickers.

Given these methodological constraints, a quantitative valuation range cannot be synthesized from the available data. The analysis reveals a structural limitation: ABL Bio's public status would normally enable revenue-based multiples, but the absence of disclosed financials precludes this approach. Key uncertainties include the scale of current revenue generation and profitability status, both essential inputs for meaningful peer-relative valuation. Practitioners should seek additional financial disclosure or employ alternative methods (DCF, sum-of-the-parts) contingent on management guidance.
